Mam 4 urządzenia od Samsunga i nigdy więcej nie kupię nic od tej firmy. Poważnie unikacjie tej firmy jak ognia. Wszystko jest ok jeżeli urządzenie działa. Natomiast w przypadku gwarancji zaczyna się zabawa. Mam obecnie otwarte jakieś 10 zgłoszeń na ich stronie, na każde odpowiada automat i sobie tak wiszą po kilka miesięcy, jak dopytuję o odpowiedź to "to zgłoszenie wymaga więcej czasu" i to jest wszystko z ich strony. Kompletny żart. Telefon ma beznadziejny zasięg od czasu zakupu. Oddawałem 3 razy na gwarancję, za każdym razem coś tam niby zrobią i dalej nie działa. Mega zbywają klientów. Jakiekolwiek customer support w tej firmie nie istnieje. Urządzenia mają wady od początku z których będą się wypierać aż wam się odechce. Zanim wpadniecie na pomysł kupienia danego sprzętu, polecam wpisać model i problem szczególnie wyszukując na zagranicznych forach. Możecie się nieźle zdziwić. Mam np. S21 i ten problem pojawia się w całej masie egzemplarzy, ilość tematów na forach pl i zagranicznych to kosmos. Co usłyszałem od Samsungowego serwisu? Że pierwszy raz mają taki problem. Jak im pokazałem ich własne forum to usłyszałem że i tak tego nie czytają. A no i akcje promocyjne, czasami w gratisie piszą że dostaniesz np. etui do tableta. Taaaa.... Ile ja się musiałem napisać żeby to dosłali, a to bo nie płacone kartą z nawzwiskiem osoby która jest na koncie samsung (serio?) a to problem z czymś tam, generalnie śmiech na sali.Podsumowując - kompletnie nie polecam.

Pozdrawiam Waldemar Kotas.

Do NOT purchase this refrigerator or any Samsung product from Samsung or any retailer.I purchased this refrigerator for my elderly parents to replace their 30+ year old refrigerator. HUGE MISTAKE!  I bought this unit from Lowe's on July 4th, 2025 and it was delivered and professionally installed on July 12th. One December 29th the ice maker stopped working. My dad, who worked in refrigeration his entire career, called the Warranty Department to issue the complaint. On the phone my 76-year old father was asked to unplug the refrigerator for 5-minutes and to find and push the reset buttons. That did not fix the issue. My father was then instructed to cut the circuit breaker at the panel. That did not fix the problem either. Please explain to me the difference between unplugging a refrigerator and cutting the circuit breaker.Lowe's / Samsung Service Department sent a Sears Repairman. The serviceman told my dad "Oh. These units are so new that Samsung has not taught us how to fix them yet"! Between my father and the serviceman they located a pinhole leak in the refrigerant line. A part was ordered and shipped to my fathers house. Three days later the repairman showed up unannounced and told my dad that he would not be installing the new part but another service person would show up and do the work. The second workman never showed up and there was no additional communication with my dad.UNACCEPTABLE!Between me and my father we have sent dozens of emails to Samsung. We have spent countless hours on the phone trying to get through to someone who could help us.Samsung issued an e-coupon to order a new refrigerator. The e-coupon was invalid. We called again and spent countless hours on the phone being transferred to unhelpful departments. Weeks later we were issued another e-coupon...there are no refrigerators in Douglas County, NV to be delivered to my parent house. Apparently there are no Samsung refrigerators available in the entire state of Nevada!!3-months later we received an email saying a refund is being issued. The refund cannot be activated using their system.Their 30+ year old refrigerator is now in their garage and works just fine.DO NOT PURCHASE Samsung products! You will never get them serviced properly and you will never get a refund!

We purchase a 65 Inch Class OLED S90F, model #QN65S90FAFXZA back in December. As of today the TV has pretty much become a $2,500 paper weight. Last night, 3/4/26, the tv was fine. Has movie night with the family and we went to bed after. Today, 3/5/26, went to turn the tv on to watch another movie with my husband and there’s a big black spot in the top center of the tv. Got in touch with Samsung via text message. I sent pictures, video, and went through their visual support tool. When going through the motions there was no color within the black spot. Tech has been do a test through the tv and the black spot started looking like the back of a CD with the rainbow affect. So I relay that information, and simply got told yes I can see it. When he got done doing whatever it was he was doing tells me that that the display has been affected by an internal impact. I asked what that means. He told me that my tv got damaged by something internal and is considered physical damage even though nothing physically hit the tv and I now have to pay out of pocket to have it repaired. The tech also could not give me pricing for repair.

I am extremely disappointed with both the quality of the oven I purchased and the appalling level of customer service I received from Samsung.The oven developed a fault after only two months and is now unusable, leaving me without a working appliance. What makes the situation even worse is the way Samsung handled the issue. Despite the fact that I had a valid receipt, had registered the product, and provided the serial number, they still refused to accept my proof of purchase. They were fully aware of the purchase details through the product registration, yet they continued to dismiss the claim.Their warranty appears to be completely worthless. As things stand, I am left with a broken oven and absolutely no indication of when the required parts will be available or when the issue might be resolved.Throughout the process, the customer service attitude was shockingly dismissive. It very much felt like a “take it or leave it” approach, with little interest in actually helping or resolving the problem.Based on this experience, I would strongly advise anyone considering a Samsung appliance to think twice. The product quality has been poor and the customer support even worse. There are plenty of other brands out there—do yourself a favour and avoid Samsung if you expect reliable products and proper after-sales support.

Disappointed in Durability and SupportWe purchased the Samsung MC17T8000CS over-the-range microwave in 2022. After less than three years of normal household use, the door latch mechanism failed. The door no longer clicks closed because the internal latch housing became loose, making the microwave unusable.This is not heavy wear-and-tear but a basic structural component failing prematurely on a mounted appliance that requires professional installation.When contacting Samsung customer support, we were told the warranty is only one year. A ticket number was created and we were referred to an authorized service company. However, they charge $120 just to diagnose the problem, before even providing a repair estimate.For a microwave that is less than three years old, this is extremely disappointing. An over-the-range unit is not inexpensive and should last far longer than this.Unfortunately, both the product durability and the post-purchase support have been disappointing.

I purchased a Samsung french door refrigerator approximately three years ago and it has experienced repeated major failures.First, the ice maker burnt out. We were initially advised to leave the fridge running even though it was making unusual noises. When we called again because the sounds seemed concerning, we were then told to switch the unit off due to a potential fire risk. When the technician eventually attended, the entire ice-making unit had melted, including the plastic housing. Food had to be discarded during this period.Approximately a year later the compressor failed. It took two weeks for a replacement compressor to be installed.Immediately after the compressor replacement, the fridge/freezer failed again. The freezer stopped freezing even though the display still showed –19 °C, and water inside the freezer remained liquid. The unit also began leaking. As a result, all food in the freezer spoiled again.We reported this via Samsung chat on Sunday and were told a technician would be notified. When we had not heard anything, we called again on Tuesday and were again assured the technician would be contacted. By Thursday we still had not been contacted. When we called the technician directly, they confirmed they had not received any service request until that day.We have recorded a conversation with Samsung in which we were told that once a technician confirms the fridge/freezer is not working, we would be eligible for a full refund and removal of the appliance. I still possess this recording.We are currently still waiting for the issue to be resolved. I would strongly discourage anyone from purchasing a fridge from this company.

This last October, my 3-year-old Samsung phone died - a battery issue caused a dead screen. I went through hell trying to replace it because logging into the Samsung site (and many other necessary sites like my bank and college websites) requires text verification. Customer service was unhelpful at best. I ended up buying another Samsung phone due to lack of other financing options. Yesterday morning, February 25th, 2026, I wake up to my new phone having been DISABLED by Samsung - only able to press a button for a customer service line. After the first few tries to get through their horrible, circular automated system, I finally got a rep and learned it was due to a bill for the amount of the trade-in credit, because I'd changed my mind on sending it in, having realized I should keep it as a back-up for future problems with my new phone. I had thought the trade-in credit would just be reversed and added to the amount I owed monthly. THE ONLY THING SAMSUNG HAD DONE TO NOTIFY ME OF THIS CHARGE WAS TO SEND A FEW EMAILS BACK IN NOVEMBER, WITH VAGUE SUBJECT LINES THAT LED ME TO BELIEVE THERE WAS A PROBLEM WITH MY MONTHLY PHONE PAYMENT THROUGH AFFIRM. They never used any other form of communication to inform me of this $150 charge, such as a text message or a physical letter, and they never even sent any further emails after November. Over three months ago! Then they thought it would be a great idea to just suddenly and completely take away my ability to use my phone, which, as we all know, is now an essential device in many aspects of our daily lives! With zero notification or warning! Over just $150!!!I had to spend FIVE HOURS on the phone with various reps yesterday, including a supervisor (who turned out to actually be LESS knowledgeable and helpful than one of the regular reps), and getting hung up on multiple times and having to start all over again, finally getting info on how to pay the $150, paying it, and being told my phone would be unlocked within 2-4 hours. Hahaha, of course it wasn't. When I again called that one number I could call from my phone, after four hours, I was told the department I needed to be transferred to was closed for the night and I had to call back this morning.But wait, there's more! When I called back this morning, thinking that at least this time the phone disabling/lock would finally be resolved quickly and easily, I was told that the rep from last night had been wrong or lied to me, that their department was the one who needed to initiate the unlocking process, and that it would take 24-72 hours. !!! I explained the whole issue yet again, how drastically it was impacting my life, got transferred to a supervisor, and now (after another hour-plus on the phone) I'm told I'll receive an email with the PIN to unlock the phone within 24 hours. Lol. I'm not holding my breath. And I'm definitely never, ever buying anything made by Samsung again. This formerly reputable company now seems to believe it can treat its customers like garbage and continue to be a profitable business, so I'm out.

I bought this item on Best Buy's website. Samsung - 17.6 cu. ft. Top-Freezer Refrigerator with FlexZone - Stainless Steel . Don't buy Samsung products, cheap quality, high price. I bought a Samsung Refrigerator in September 2023 for $950.00 from Best Buy's website because I wanted to replace my white Kenmore Refrigerator with a Stainless Steel refrigerator. On January 2026, the top freezer and bottom refrigerator completely stopped cooling costing me hundreds in loss of food and major inconvenience, the refrigerator was down for a week. Luckily, I still had my white Kenmore Refrigerator in the garage and was able to still have a refrigerator I could use but had to go up and down stairs for every item I needed. I called Samsung customer service and they sent a Technician out which took a couple days and the technician said that the Inverter Board and Invertor compressor had gone bad. I was charged $140 for the diagnosis and another $150 to replace the damaged parts. I requested that Samsung cover the costs since this was clearly manufacturer defects and poor quality if after only 2 years the Refrigerator already stopped working? Ridiculous. I requested 2 times that a Samsung manager call me to refund me the cost for the repair and no one ever called me. I will never buy Samsung products again. I have always bought Kenmore Refrigerators and never had a problem with their quality, in fact the Kenmore still works great after 10 years!

I have been a long-time Samsung customer, purchasing everything from televisions to mobile phones based on their reputation for quality products and customer service. On January 9, 2026, I purchased a Galaxy A16 5G (128GB). I was surprised afterwards to discover that the phone did not include a power adapter—only a cable. Had this been clearly disclosed in the description, I would not have made the purchase. Adapters ALWAYS came with their phones. If they suddenly made this change then they need to state CLEARLY (not hidden) that Adapters are sold separately and phone needs adapter or a charging source to work. Charging the phone via a cable connected to an extension cord takes 4-5 hours. Some senior consumers don't have anything and must have the added expense. I attempted to purchase a Samsung adapter from a third-party seller who claimed it came directly from Samsung distribution, only to receive an empty, supposedly sealed package. Multiple calls to Samsung customer service have been unsuccessful. I was promised supervisor callbacks that never occurred and was ultimately disconnected after requesting further assistance. Today I was disconnected again. I am very disappointed in Samsung's customer service. This situation has become unnecessarily frustrating for something as basic and inexpensive as a power adapter. This multi-billion dollar company needs to go out of business just for their neglect of customer service alone. They just don't care.

I purchased four expensive items on January 22nd. Three arrived within two days, but the fourth item—an American fridge freezer—was scheduled for delivery the following Wednesday, which I understood due to its size.On the first delivery attempt, the team discovered the doors needed to be removed to bring it inside. They attempted this using an impact driver, which damaged the screws, and the fridge had to be returned for a replacement.A week later, on the second delivery date, I was informed the delivery van had broken down. The fridge eventually arrived after a van change, but it was already dark and the delivery team had no trolley or tools (due to the earlier van swap). They advised me to reschedule to avoid damaging the fridge.Two days later, on the third delivery attempt, the team unpacked the fridge only to find a large scratch on one of the doors. The item was returned again, likely damaged during repeated loading and unloading.I was told I would be contacted within 48 hours with next steps. After hearing nothing, I contacted Samsung myself. I asked to speak with a supervisor but was told no one was available and that I would receive a call within 24–48 hours.Two days later, still having received no call, I contacted Samsung again—only to be told there was no record whatsoever of my previous request for a supervisor to call me back. This was extremely frustrating, particularly given the ongoing delays.When I called again, I was offered only a £30 Samsung voucher and told I would receive an updated delivery date as soon as possible. It is now Thursday February 19th, and I am still without a fridge and still waiting for a confirmed delivery date.

I used to absolutely love Samsung products, which makes this experience even more disappointing. My Samsung Galaxy S24 (512GB) has had issues from day one. The phone randomly shut down and restarted on its own, and for the past 22 months I’ve dealt with constant reboots. In the last few days, it has escalated into a nonstop boot loop, day and night, making the phone completely unusable.I contacted Samsung support multiple times over the years. While the representatives were always polite, I was never actually given a real solution — nothing like the level of troubleshooting and effective support I consistently receive when I call Apple about my iPhone. Those teams truly know how to diagnose and fix issues. We purchased a brand-new Samsung refrigerator directly from Samsung. Within 36 hours of delivery and installation by Samsung reps, the refrigerator catastrophically failed due to a defective ice maker and leaked water throughout the first level of our home. Samsung acknowledged responsibility and ultimately transferred the matter to its insurance carrier. An independent inspection confirmed damage consistent with the reported loss. We promptly hired a professional mitigation company to prevent mold and further damage and paid those costs out of pocket. Despite acknowledged liability, completed inspections, and full cooperation on our part, Samsung and its insurer delayed resolution for more than 80 days. During this time, our kitchen and dining room remained unusable, personal property was damaged, and our family endured prolonged disruption over multiple holidays. We were repeatedly asked for additional documentation, estimates, and diagnostics, some at Samsung’s direction, only to be told later that those costs were “the homeowner’s responsibility.” Samsung’s insurer offered a settlement that would leave our home partially repaired, mismatched, and below its pre-loss condition, while requiring a broad release of future rights. What should have been a straightforward resolution of damage caused by a defective product turned into an exhausting, protracted process marked by delay, cost-shifting, and undervaluation. We deeply regret purchasing this product and caution other consumers to consider not only the appliance itself, but how Samsung handles serious problems when things go wrong.

Let me start by saying I don’t write a review if it’s not 5 star. Everyone has a bad day or a judgement/moral slip up from time to time. This experience however was over 2 months and I truly feel the employees are trained to defer, lie, place you on hold for hours and stall until the customer gives up and decides it just isn’t worth their time. Such a frustrating experience. I bought a dryer in March of 2025. I chose to purchase on Samsung.com to avoid the middle man of a 3rd party like Home Depot. The dryer has stopped working 3 times in 9 months. Samsung has sent a tech out each time to fix it. This last time I called bc it has been the same issue every time and the techs have all told me it will continue to happen. With the 1 year warranty coming up I called and asked to return the dryer bc this is clearly a lemon. After LITERAL hours of being passed from one dept to another, I finally got someone that said yes, a refund or exchange is warranted. You would think the saga ended happily but that was 2 months ago. I was told I would receive a text that i would just need to click a link and sign a terms and condition page. Easy. However, the texting has continued for 2 months. They continue to drag it on sending me links that don’t work, requesting pics and receipts that I have provided 6 times now and only responding every 3-6 days. They even asked me to peel off the sticker inside the dryer with the SKU and take a pic of it on the counter. I did so. Then the rep asked me to throw it away. Given the history I now have had with them, I THANKFULLY put it back on the dryer against their instructions. 2 WEEKS later, they text and said the first picture wasn’t clear (which it was)so they would need a diff pic of the sticker on a diff surface and if I couldn’t provide that, then they couldn’t process the exchange. I mean….. they also continued to text a number they supposedly had on file that wasn’t mine and told me there isn’t a way to update a phone number in their system. So here we are…2 months later…hours and hours of sitting on hold, being passed from one dept to another and I am once again on hold, the timer on my phone says I have been on the call for 48 min and still no refund. Still getting bounced btwn reps and getting no answers. I feel like I am in a prank show and hopefully I win a million dollars for the 16 hours I have spent dealing with this scam of a company.And on top of all that, and at this point, this is just a side note…the last tech that came out, unplugged the washing machine while working on the dryer and flooded the house. To his credit, he took full responsibility and Samsungs 3rd party tech company, One Stop Appliance, is covering the repairs for 165 sq ft of wood flooring. Im keeping a positive mindset that they will be more honest than Samsung, but time will tell.

So we ordered...Tv arrived, fitter came and, no...it needed to be channelled in, as in-fact, there were wires! This was for over a fireplace so this was not an option, hence the need for a 'wireless' box!!!Rang Samsung and now find that we need to pay £50 for it to be collected!Went back online did a micro search again of website and found in the depths of the info (last item no 14) that it said "no wires shown"???Samsung your tech services, marketing and website let us down and you are totally misleading the customer! Disgraceful and time wasting!!!

Samsung Z Flip Series Complaint: "Bent Frame" Excuse vs. Chronic Hinge FailureI am sharing this to warn potential users of the Samsung Z Flip series about the service approach they may encounter after purchase.I sent my Samsung Z Flip series device, which is still under warranty, to the authorized service center due to the hinge sticking and failing to open fully, which is the most notorious weak point of this series. Despite no drops, impacts, or visible damage, the authorized service center Bilişim Destek Hizmetleri San. Tic. A.Ş. in Maltepe/İstanbul issued a repair quote of 15,260 TRY (around 450 USD) under work order number PR00691458 – 4291622525, using the questionable and completely subjective excuse of a “bent frame.”The fundamental issue here is not just my individual device; it is the systematic approach of Samsung authorized services to label chronic and production-based mechanical wear on foldable screens and hinges as “user error.” While these devices are marketed as technological marvels at the point of sale, they are conveniently excluded from warranty coverage by leveraging the slightest cosmetic usage marks as excuses when it comes to service.Because I refused this unjustified repair fee, even basic maintenance and parts replacements that should normally be covered under warranty were denied, effectively locking the entire process. As a result, I have initiated the Consumer Arbitration Committee (legal action) process.For those considering the Samsung Z Flip series, I must emphasize that this technology is not sufficiently backed by service and warranty reliability when facing chronic issues. No matter how carefully you use your device, you may find yourself completely unsupported when a production- or design-related problem occurs.

I originally bought a basic Samsung speaker because I loved its design. After inspecting the device, they confirmed it needed to be replaced. However, a few days later, they called to say the model was out of stock in Türkiye. They offered a refund, but I turned it down; I really liked that specific speaker and told them I was happy to wait until they had more in stock.Three weeks later, they called again to explain that there were ongoing supply issues. They offered to swap my speaker for a different sound system instead. Feeling a bit disappointed but realizing I needed a working device, I accepted the offer without looking too closely at the link they sent me.When the new system arrived and I set it up to watch a Dolby Atmos movie, I couldn't believe my ears especially the power of the woofer. When I checked the model details, I realized Samsung had sent me their flagship home theater system!I am still in shock. This was such a generous gesture and the best customer service experience I’ve ever had. Because of this treatment, I’ve decided to stick with Samsung for all my future home appliances and mobile phones.

I bought a Samsung washing machine directly from Samsung in July 2023. The order confirmation email clearly stated “5 year guarantee”. The machine then failed after about 2.5 years, the display would not even light up, although it was clearly receiving power.What followed has been a genuinely awful customer experience. The moment something went wrong, I was passed from pillar to post across live chat, phone, and email. Every interaction felt scripted. I kept getting the same empty phrases like “we understand” and “I’m taking a note”, but no one seemed to have the authority to actually resolve anything. I repeatedly asked a basic question. If Samsung is now saying the five year guarantee was conditional on registration within 90 days, why does the order confirmation itself just say “5 year guarantee” with no clear warning or requirement. No one could answer that in a meaningful way.The only “resolution” offered was a 20% discount. That was insulting for an appliance that has died at 2.5 years, especially when it was sold with a five year guarantee shown on the purchase confirmation. It also felt like a default, script driven offer that they give anyone who contacts live chat. From what I have seen others say on Reddit and similar forums, people sometimes get offered more, which is honestly the only practical advice I can give. Push for better, do not accept the first number. In my case, the person who finally spoke to me sounded half asleep and like they could not be bothered to actually take ownership and get anything approved.It took five days to speak to someone who sounded remotely senior, and even then they just repeated the same lines as the person I spoke to on day one.If you are buying from Samsung, be prepared. Once there is a problem you may be stuck dealing with a customer service process that goes nowhere. Based on this experience, I have lost confidence in both the durability of Samsung appliances and the company’s willingness to stand behind what it advertises.

I rarely write reviews, but my experience with Samsung appliances — specifically the Samsung 6.0 cu ft Freestanding Gas Range (Model NX60T8111SS/AA) — has been so consistently bad that I need to warn other buyers.I purchased this range in April 2023, and within just a few months the main control board failed. Samsung sent Sears to replace it. Less than a year later, the same board failed again, and Samsung had to send Sears out for a second replacement.Once the unit fell out of warranty, the problems only got worse. In summer 2025, I had to replace the main board for a third time, along with the keypad. Now it’s December 2025, and unbelievably, the main board has failed yet again — making this the fourth time in just 2.5 years that this critical component has needed replacement.This is unacceptable. No major appliance should require full control-board replacements every few months. It is clearly a defective product and should be treated as a lemon. The cost, the inconvenience, and the repeated failures have been beyond frustrating.After this experience, I will never purchase another Samsung appliance again. The quality control is simply not there, and the repeated failures of the same part make it clear that this design is fundamentally flawed.Buyers beware.
